fix: Phase 1 & 2 triage workflow (6 critical bugs) (#4)

* fix(extract): use click.echo to avoid Rich line wrapping of filenames

* fix(move): resolve destinations relative to vault root when --source-dir is used

* fix(fetch): skip previously trashed URLs in RSS duplicate filter

* fix(fetch): catch RSS feed errors to avoid aborting all feeds

* fix(fetch): recover destination URL from truncated tracking URLs after HTTP 400

* fix(preprocess): add actionable guidance for frontmatter errors

* docs: update CHANGELOG and TODO for Phase 1 triage workflow fixes

* docs: remove non-existent --auto-remove-dupes flag from README examples

* fix(move): resolve cache path relative to vault root, not source dir

When --source-dir is a relative subdirectory (e.g., Inbox/), cache should
still be loaded from the vault root to maintain consistency with other cache
consumers and ensure duplicate tracking works correctly across subdirectories.

Addresses Copilot review comment.

* fix(preprocess): capitalize Unicode in error message

* fix(changelog): clarify RSS feed error handling behavior

* fix(move): normalize relative source-dir paths and detect subdirectories

* fix(fetch): handle percent-encoded URLs in tracking destination recovery

* fix(cache): restrict duplicate skip behavior to RSS feeds only

* fix(fetch): include RSS error in JSON output format

* test(fetch): add test coverage for tracking URL recovery

* fix: address additional Copilot PR review comments

- fix(fetch): output JSON on RSS error for structured error handling
- fix(move): remove exists=True validation and add manual check after normalization
- test(fetcher): improve malformed encoding test to reflect lenient unquote behavior

* fix(cache): handle absolute cache paths correctly in filter_duplicate_urls and update_cache_after_fetch

* fix: address final Copilot PR review comments

- fix(fetcher): make percent-encoded URL pattern case-insensitive
- fix(fetcher): remove broad exception handler from unquote (not needed)
- fix(fetcher): simplify HTTPError exception handling (RequestError is subclass)
- fix(move): use click.BadParameter for consistent error handling
- test(fetcher): add test case for lowercase percent-encoded URLs

* fix: address Copilot review comments and proactively fix similar patterns

- fix(move): add is_dir() check after exists() to reject file paths passed as --source-dir
- fix(fetch): use click.echo for JSON output to avoid Rich line-wrapping
- fix(duplicates): use click.echo for structured output to avoid Rich line-wrapping
- fix(stats): use click.echo for JSON/YAML output to avoid Rich line-wrapping

* fix: address Copilot review comments and proactively fix similar patterns

- fix(move): resolve paths before relative_to() containment check to prevent
  .. traversal bypass (e.g. root/../outside would escape vault root lexically)
- fix(fetch): include recovered URL in HTTP 400 error when tracking URL
  recovery is attempted but fails, for actionable debugging context
- fix(fetch): include failing feed URL in rss_error message so CLI and JSON
  consumers can identify which feed failed without extra logging
- fix(mover): use keyword argument for dest_root in execute_move() and
  _update_cache_after_moves() calls to prevent order-dependent bugs

* fix(fetch): include recovery exception details in HTTP 400 error message

When tracking URL recovery is attempted but fails, the error now includes
both the recovered URL and the underlying exception (e.g. HTTP 403, timeout)
so users can diagnose why the retry failed without extra logging.

* test(move): add CLI test for relative --source-dir destination rooting

Add TestMoveRelativeSourceDir to verify that when --source-dir is a
relative path (e.g. Inbox), files are moved to vault_root/Category/
rather than vault_root/Inbox/Category/.

Also remove now-unused type: ignore[assert-type] comment on the
source_dir None assertion in move command.

commit.message:
chore: add go.work to support multi-module workspace (#1618)

Running `go mod tidy` in the root inadvertently affects `website/go.mod`
and `website/go.sum` because the Go toolchain doesn't recognize the
nested module without an explicit workspace declaration. This also
breaks `gopls` language server support across both modules.

## Changes

- **Add `go.work`** at the repository root declaring both modules as
workspace members:

```go
go 1.24.0

use (
    .
    ./website
)
```

This scopes `go mod tidy` to each module independently and enables
correct multi-module analysis in editors. For website dependency updates
(e.g. Docsy), use `hugo mod tidy` / `hugo mod get -u` inside `website/`
rather than `go mod tidy`.

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Chore: Add go.work to support multi-module workspace
(root and website)</issue_title>
> <issue_description>### Background
> This repository currently contains two separate Go modules:
> 1. The main application module in the root (`manael.org/x/manael/v2`)
> 2. The documentation website module in the `website/` directory
(`manael.org/website`), which uses Hugo Modules to manage the Docsy
theme dependencies.
> 
> Currently, running `go mod tidy` in the root directory can cause
unintended side effects (such as wiping out or ignoring the
`website/go.mod` and `website/go.sum` files). This happens because the
standard Go toolchain does not automatically recognize the nested module
as part of the same development project. This also negatively affects
editor integrations like `gopls` in VS Code, which may fail to provide
correct language server features for both modules simultaneously.
> 
> ### Proposal
> Introduce a `go.work` (Go Workspace) file in the root directory. This
explicitly tells the Go toolchain, editors, and dependency bots (like
Renovate) that this repository contains multiple independent modules
that should be developed and analyzed together as a unified workspace.
> 
> ### Tasks
> - Create a `go.work` file in the repository root by running the
following commands:
>   ```bash
>   go work init
>   go work use .
>   go work use ./website
>   ```
> - Commit the generated `go.work` file to the repository.
> 
> ### Example `go.work`
> ```go
> go 1.24.0
> 
> use (
> 	.
> 	./website
> )
> ```
> 
> ### Note for Hugo Development
> When updating dependencies for the website (e.g., the Docsy theme),
developers should run `hugo mod tidy` or `hugo mod get -u` inside the
`website/` directory, rather than using the standard `go mod
tidy`.</issue_description>

fix: prevent URL navigation when RiSc deletion requires approval (#905)

When deleting a RiSc that requires approval, the onSuccess callback
(which navigates to the first RiSc) was always called regardless of
whether the deletion was complete or merely sent for approval.

This caused the URL to immediately change to the first RiSc even though
the deleted RiSc still existed in the list with status
DeletionSentForApproval, making the UI misleading during multi-step
deletion flows.

Fix: move the onSuccess call inside the ProcessingStatus.DeletedRiSc
branch so navigation only occurs when the RiSc is actually deleted.

[Mgmt Emitter] Fix cross-scope parent detection for LegacyOperations resources (#56800)

* [Mgmt Generator] Fix list operation routing for cross-scope parent resources

When a resource has a tenant-scoped list operation but a subscription-scoped
parent (or vice versa), the list operation was incorrectly routed to extension
methods instead of the collection. This happened because the list operation's
resourceScope (tenant path) didn't exactly match the parentResourceId
(subscription path).

Added a fallback check in CategorizeMethods: when the exact scope match fails,
verify if the operation path structurally matches the resource's ID pattern
minus the last segment (i.e., it IS a 'list children' path for the resource).
If so, route it to the collection where it belongs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix cross-scope parent detection in emitter, not generator

Move the fix from generator routing (ResourceMetadataExtensions.cs) to the
emitter's postProcessArmResources. When LegacyOperations creates the same
model at multiple scopes (e.g., subscription and tenant), the path-based
parent detection in buildArmProviderSchema correctly identifies scope-matching
parents. However, postProcessArmResources was overwriting these with the first
decorator-based match (from @parentResource), which could pick the wrong scope.

The fix: skip overwriting parentResourceId in postProcessArmResources if it
was already set by the caller's path-based detection.

Added emitter test for cross-scope LegacyOperations parent assignment.
Reverted generator-side workaround (IsListChildrenPath).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use SQLITE_DB_PATH to avoid collision with Railway's DATABASE_URL

Railway auto-injects DATABASE_URL as a PostgreSQL connection string.
Using it as a SQLite file path crashed the server on startup, causing
health checks to fail. Renamed to SQLITE_DB_PATH and defaulted to
/data/standupper.db to align with the Railway volume mount at /data.

- architecture: coupling, complexity, SOLID
- no_slop: dead code, placeholders, copy-paste
- fallback: error handling, no silent failures
- honesty: comments match code, no lies
- mock_data: no hardcoded/fake data in prod
- hardcoded: no magic numbers, no hardcoded URLs/credentials
- test_quality: coverage ≥80%, real assertions, no false tests
- over_engineering: YAGNI, no premature abstraction
- observability: logging, metrics, health checks
- resilience: retry, timeout, graceful degradation
- traceability: feature→story→code→test 100% refs

New DB tables: ac_adversarial, ac_traceability
New columns: ac_cycles.adversarial_scores, ac_cycles.traceability_score
New endpoints: GET /api/improvement/adversarial/{id}, GET /api/improvement/traceability/{id}

Dashboard now has 7 tabs:
Vue globale | Cycle actuel | ⚔️ Adversarial | 🔗 Traçabilité | Agents | Historique | Live

fix: resolve "Failed to update user" error in UserManagement (#77)

* Initial plan

* fix: resolve Failed to update user error in UserManagement

- Edge function: make auth.users metadata update non-fatal when only
  role/location/name are being synced; only abort on email/password
  auth failures. This prevents transient auth-API errors from blocking
  the public.users profile update (the source of truth).
- Edge function: add fallback error strings so error responses always
  carry a non-empty `error` field (avoids JSON.stringify dropping
  undefined values and returning `{}`).
- userService.updateUser: also check `result.message` as fallback so
  Supabase infrastructure errors (which use `message` not `error`) are
  surfaced correctly instead of falling back to the generic string.
- UserManagement.jsx: add editable `name` input to the inline edit row,
  include trimmed name in the save payload, add client-side validation
  for the required name field, and surface the real server error message
  instead of a hard-coded string. Refactor repeated editingUser?.id
  check into a local `isEditing` variable for clarity.

commit.message:
Enhance file management, download logic, and comment features (#531)

This pull request introduces enhancements to file download handling and
improves filter injection for dynamic table and chart dialogs in the Vue
components. The most significant changes are grouped below by theme.

**File download functionality:**

* Added a new API endpoint `download_file` in
`frappe_theme/apis/file_manager.py` to handle file downloads using a
stream, setting the correct filename and response type for downloading
files.
* Updated the iframe download handler in `App.vue` to use `window.open`
for downloads instead of creating and clicking an anchor element,
improving reliability of file downloads.

**Dynamic filter injection for dialogs:**

* Enhanced `NumberCard.vue` and `Chart.vue` to dynamically fetch and
inject filters using the `dt_events` property from the form (`frm`),
allowing pre-filters to be combined with user filters for both report
and document type dialogs.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-ec302968b8de1a1b58f4aae395de3e0032ea32ff03b8a825bda741c75b0db2d7R109-R136)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-c8aa9f8e1911cc77239f665dcd8ac953d2510e31301d4ea941127666aed95bfbR380-R407)
* Added logic to set `dt_events` with a `get_filters` function in both
`NumberCard.vue` and `Chart.vue`, ensuring that dialogs have access to
the correct filter sets based on card or chart details.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-ec302968b8de1a1b58f4aae395de3e0032ea32ff03b8a825bda741c75b0db2d7R164-R170)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-c8aa9f8e1911cc77239f665dcd8ac953d2510e31301d4ea941127666aed95bfbR431-R437)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-ec302968b8de1a1b58f4aae395de3e0032ea32ff03b8a825bda741c75b0db2d7R187-R193)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-c8aa9f8e1911cc77239f665dcd8ac953d2510e31301d4ea941127666aed95bfbR453-R459)

**Minor code improvements:**

* Fixed usage of optional chaining when passing the `doc` property in
`App.vue` to ensure correct parameter handling.

- Add .AsNoTracking() to all read-only queries in CatService and TodoService
  for a consistent performance pattern (avoids unnecessary change-tracking
  overhead even when each method creates a short-lived DbContext)
- Reduce Cat.Name from nvarchar(200) → nvarchar(100)
- Reduce Todo.Title from nvarchar(500) → nvarchar(200)

  (previously nvarchar(max), which is wasteful for name fields)
- New migration: TightenColumnLengths

⚠️ Data-loss risk: any existing Cat.Name > 100 chars or Todo.Title > 200 chars
